scope: Direct and adjacent competitors to The Commons — a Singapore-based peer-to-peer event organisation platform with built-in escrow payments (funds held until event delivery), a 2-sided pro services marketplace (photographers, caterers, MCs, decorators), and a daiquiri-beach themed UX backed by Elitez Group. Set covers global and SG ticketing platforms, experience marketplaces, space rental platforms, freelance/gig marketplaces used for event vendor sourcing, payment & escrow services, and the silent-majority DIY stack (Google Form + PayNow + WhatsApp).
methodology: Public pricing pages, organiser-facing fee schedules, and SG-localisation feature audits as of Apr 2026. Where fees are tiered, the mid-tier rate is used. SG active-organiser counts triangulated from Peatix SG public event counts, Eventbrite SG landing-page event density, Klook SG inventory counts, LinkedIn HQ data, STB 2025 sector reports, e27 and Tech in Asia coverage. Escrow flag = does the platform hold funds until event delivery (vs pay-out-on-sale or T+7). Marketplace flag = is there a genuine 2-sided supply side (vendors list services). sg_localisation_score: +1 each for PayNow/PayLah, SGD-default pricing, SG GST/IRAS invoice-ready, SG phone/CS support, multi-language UI.

competitors.name: Eventbrite
competitors.target_market: SG semi-pro organisers running 50-500 seat paid events (conferences, classes, nightlife) wanting global discovery + email marketing
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: ~10.7% all-in (3.7% + S$1.59/ticket service + 3.5% processing on a S$50 ticket)
strengths: Massive organic discovery via eventbrite.sg domain authority
Mature organiser tooling: recurring events, promo codes, waitlists, check-in app
Native integrations with Mailchimp, HubSpot, Salesforce
weaknesses: Funds paid out T+4-7 business days AFTER event — not true escrow; buyer has no recourse if organiser cancels last-minute
Compounded fees brutal on S$20-40 tickets — small workshops priced out
No vendor / freelancer marketplace — organisers must source photographers, caterers, MCs elsewhere

competitors.name: Peatix
competitors.target_market: SG community organisers, language meetups, hobby groups running 30-200 seat events at affordable prices
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: 4.9% + S$0.99/ticket on paid events; free events are free
strengths: Strong SG community footprint — language exchanges, photography meetups, parenting groups
Lower fees than Eventbrite at every price tier
Mobile-first UX with native apps
weaknesses: Pay-out on T+30 after event — slower than Eventbrite, no escrow buyer-protection
Discovery surface inferior to Eventbrite SEO authority
No vendor marketplace; no PayNow native

competitors.name: Meetup
competitors.target_market: SG hobby and interest-group organisers running recurring free or low-cost meetups
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Organiser pays ~US$24/mo subscription; free for attendees; no escrow
strengths: Strong recurring-group muscle memory among 30-50 yr old SG professionals
Discovery via interest taxonomy (running, photography, board games)
Subscription model — no per-ticket fee gouge
weaknesses: Almost zero payment infrastructure — organisers chase attendees on PayNow / Venmo manually
No escrow, no refund protection, no dispute resolution
Group dues collection is the #1 organiser pain — Meetup does not solve it

competitors.name: Klook
competitors.target_market: SG residents and inbound tourists booking curated experiences, attractions, day-trips, classes (S$30-300 per booking)
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: ~15-25% commission baked into price (operator-side, not consumer-disclosed)
strengths: Best-in-class mobile booking UX in SE Asia for experiences
Curated 2-sided marketplace — Klook qualifies operators, attendees trust the brand
Massive consumer brand, SGD-default, PayNow + cards native
weaknesses: Closed marketplace — peer creators cannot self-list a one-off birthday party or hobby workshop
Commission opaque to consumer; operator-side fees are 15-25% (vs The Commons P2P transparency)
Built for tourism / repeat experiences, not one-off P2P events

competitors.name: SISTIC
competitors.target_market: SG enterprise venues, performing arts centres, large-format ticketed productions (Esplanade, MBS, Star Theatre)
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Attendee pays ~S$4 booking fee/ticket + ~3% processing; organiser commercials negotiated
strengths: Default for SG performing arts and large-format venues
Trusted SG consumer brand for event ticket purchase
Allocation, reserved seating, anti-scalping mature
weaknesses: Not self-serve — small organisers cannot list themselves
Brochure-ware website, dated UX, no marketplace surface
No escrow protection; no SG vendor marketplace

competitors.name: EventNook
competitors.target_market: SG corporate event organisers, conferences, training providers wanting GST-compliant invoicing
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: 5% + S$0.50/ticket + 3% processing on paid; SaaS plans from S$99/mo for unlimited free events
strengths: SG-built — GST/IRAS-ready invoicing out of box
Custom registration forms popular with corporate / training events
PayNow accepted natively
weaknesses: Discovery surface near zero — organisers must drive their own traffic
No vendor marketplace; no escrow buyer-protection
B2B-leaning, weak P2P / community-event muscle

competitors.name: Funzing
competitors.target_market: Urban professionals 25-45 booking unconventional experiences (lectures, supper clubs, workshops); minor SG presence
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: ~20% commission baked in (host-side)
strengths: Curated quirky-experience brand voice
Open host onboarding — peers can list
Strong UK / EU community
weaknesses: No SG operations footprint
Commission ~20% — heavier than The Commons 5%
No PayNow, no escrow

competitors.name: Airbnb Experiences
competitors.target_market: Hosts running curated experiences for Airbnb's traveller audience; SG hosts running food walks, kayak trips, etc.
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: 20% host service fee + payment processing
strengths: Massive global audience and trust halo
Curated host quality bar
Best-in-class experience-product UX
weaknesses: Travel-only audience — no domestic P2P birthday/hobby utility
20% commission
No escrow / no SG-localised payment rails

competitors.name: Venuerific
competitors.target_market: SG event organisers searching for venues — birthdays, corporate offsites, weddings, photo shoots
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Lead-gen / commission to venue (not disclosed); free for organiser to enquire
strengths: Default SG venue-discovery surface
Wide inventory across CBD, heartland, F&B, arts spaces
Strong SEO for venue queries
weaknesses: Not transactional — bookings happen offline after enquiry
No escrow, no vendor marketplace beyond venues
Commission opaque, no peer-organiser tooling

competitors.name: Peerspace
competitors.target_market: Creators and event organisers booking unique spaces by the hour for shoots, offsites, parties (US/EU primary)
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: ~15% host fee + 5% guest service fee, processing inclusive
strengths: Hourly-rental + escrow model is the gold standard for trust
Strong 2-sided marketplace dynamics
Insurance bundled into booking
weaknesses: No SG inventory of meaningful scale
USD-priced; no PayNow
Spaces only — no event-vendor marketplace for photographers, caterers

competitors.name: Stripe Connect
competitors.target_market: Marketplace builders needing programmable payment splits, delayed payouts, and KYC-compliant payouts to vendors
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: 3.4% + S$0.50 / card; programmable hold-and-release for escrow flows
strengths: True programmable escrow primitives — hold funds until release event
Marketplace payment compliance handled (KYC, payouts)
Best developer experience in payments globally
weaknesses: Not a consumer / organiser product — pure infrastructure
No discovery, no UI, no vendor marketplace
The Commons builds on top of this — Stripe Connect is enabler, not direct competitor

competitors.name: Trustap
competitors.target_market: P2P transactions where both sides need escrow protection — concert resale tickets, marketplace goods, freelance services
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Tiered: ~3.95% + small fixed fee per transaction, paid by buyer or split
strengths: Purpose-built P2P escrow with dispute resolution
Multi-currency, including SGD
Embedded SDK for marketplaces to integrate
weaknesses: No event discovery, no organiser tooling, no vendor marketplace
Niche awareness in SG
Generic escrow — no event-day delivery semantics

competitors.name: Escrow.com
competitors.target_market: High-value B2B and asset transactions (domains, vehicles, art) needing licensed escrow agent
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: 0.89% to 3.25% sliding by transaction size; ~S$25 minimum
strengths: Licensed escrow agent — strongest legal posture
Trusted by domain-name and high-asset transaction community
Pure escrow primitive
weaknesses: Built for high-value asset deals, not S$30 birthday tickets
No event discovery, no vendor marketplace
Workflow too heavy for P2P event use case

competitors.name: PayNow / PayLah!
competitors.target_market: Every SG bank account holder — used for P2P transfer including event collection by organisers
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Free for personal P2P; corporate PayNow ~0.5% per transaction
strengths: Universal SG adoption — every banked SG resident
Instant settlement, free for personal use
QR collection muscle memory established
weaknesses: Zero escrow — funds settle instantly to organiser; attendee has no recourse if event cancels
No event discovery, no headcount tracking, no vendor marketplace
DIY workflow forces organisers into spreadsheet + WhatsApp chase mode

competitors.name: Splitwise
competitors.target_market: Friend groups splitting expenses — trips, group dinners, shared rent, shared events
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Free for tracking; no payment rail in SG (US Venmo only)
strengths: Strong group-expense tracking primitive
Mobile-first UX
Familiar to most SG digital natives
weaknesses: Tracks IOUs but does not collect — organiser still chases on PayNow
No escrow, no event ticketing, no vendor marketplace
Tracks debts, doesn't deliver events

competitors.name: Fiverr
competitors.target_market: Buyers sourcing low-cost freelance services — design, video, copy; some event-vendor categories (MC, video editor)
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: 20% seller fee + 5.5% buyer service fee + S$3 small-order fee
strengths: Built-in escrow — funds released on buyer approval
Mature 2-sided marketplace dynamics
Wide vendor base, including some SG event-vendor categories
weaknesses: Generic freelance marketplace, not event-specialised
20% seller fee + 5.5% buyer fee = ~25% all-in
Vendors are global; SG-day-of-event reliability uneven

competitors.name: Bark.com
competitors.target_market: Buyers requesting quotes for local services — event vendors, photographers, caterers, MCs, party rentals
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Vendors pay per-lead credits (~S$10-40); buyer side is free
strengths: SG vendor coverage for events / parties / catering / photography
Buyer-side is free
Lead-form workflow familiar
weaknesses: Adversarial UX for vendors (paid leads, low conversion)
No escrow, no transactional layer — bookings happen offline
Reputation damage in vendor community

competitors.name: Carousell Services
competitors.target_market: SG buyers looking for ad-hoc services — birthday clowns, party photographers, MCs, helium balloon delivery
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: CarouPay ~3% + S$0.50; listing free; protected payments optional
strengths: Massive SG user base across all categories
Native PayNow alternative (CarouPay) with escrow option
Strong mobile-first SG brand
weaknesses: Generic classifieds — not event-specialised
Service quality very variable; no organiser tooling
No event-discovery layer

competitors.name: FunEmpire
competitors.target_market: SG corporate teams and birthday hosts booking team-bonding activities, escape rooms, virtual games
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: ~15-25% commission baked into activity price
strengths: Strong SG corporate-bonding brand
Curated activity catalogue, fewer-but-vetted vendors
PayNow + corporate invoicing supported
weaknesses: Curated, closed marketplace — peers cannot self-list
Corporate-bonding focus, not P2P birthday / community events
No escrow, opaque commission

competitors.name: Luma
competitors.target_market: Tech-leaning organisers running 50-500 person community events, conferences, hackathons; rapidly growing in SG tech scene
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Free for free events; ~5% + processing on paid
strengths: Best-in-class event UX — calendar invites, email RSVPs, social proof
Lower fees than Eventbrite
Rapid adoption in SG tech / VC / hackathon community
weaknesses: No escrow — instant pay-out to organiser
No vendor marketplace
USD-leaning, weak GST/IRAS posture for SG corporate events

competitors.name: Humanitix
competitors.target_market: Charity-aligned organisers — community fundraisers, cultural festivals, school events; growing in AU/SG
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Booking fees comparable to Eventbrite; 100% of profits go to children's-education charity
strengths: Charity-aligned narrative — strong with values-driven organisers
Comparable feature set to Eventbrite
AU-built with mature ANZ presence
weaknesses: No SG localisation — no PayNow, weak SG SEO
No escrow, no vendor marketplace
Charity narrative is the differentiator; product itself is parity

competitors.name: DIY: Google Form + PayNow + WhatsApp
competitors.target_market: The silent majority of SG P2P events — birthdays, hobby meetups, group trips run by anyone with a phone
competitors.estimated_platform_fee_sgd: Free in money; high in time — organiser averages 4-8 hours chasing per event
strengths: Zero monetary cost
Complete organiser control
Universal — every SG resident already has Forms + PayNow + WhatsApp
weaknesses: Organiser does the work of a platform — chasing PayNow screenshots, reconciling, refunding
No escrow — attendees have no recourse if event cancels
Headcount drift, last-minute cancellations, no marketplace for vendors

categories.global_ticketing: Large international ticketing platforms — Eventbrite, Luma, Humanitix. Mature event tooling, weak escrow, no vendor marketplace.
categories.sea_ticketing: SG / SE Asia ticketing — Peatix, SISTIC, EventNook. Stronger SG localisation, weaker discovery, no escrow.
categories.experience_marketplace: Curated experience marketplaces — Klook, Airbnb Experiences, FunEmpire, Funzing. 2-sided but closed; built for repeat tourism / corporate bonding.
categories.space_rental: Venue and space rental — Venuerific, Peerspace. Adjacent — solve the venue problem, not the event-organisation problem.
categories.community_newsletter: Community / interest-group platforms — Meetup. Strong recurring-group muscle, weak payment infrastructure.
categories.freelance_marketplace: Freelance / gig platforms used for event vendor sourcing — Fiverr, Bark, Carousell Services. Generic; not event-specialised.
categories.payment_escrow: Payment + escrow primitives — Stripe Connect, Trustap, Escrow.com, PayNow, Splitwise. Pure rails; no event tooling.
categories.diy_alt: The silent-majority DIY stack — Google Form + PayNow + WhatsApp. Free in money, expensive in organiser time.

landscape.marketSize: SG events sector estimated at S$1.2B annually (STB 2025 leisure events + corporate MICE + community events). P2P / community / hobby-event share ~S$280M (~23%) — the slice The Commons targets, currently under-served by transactional platforms.
landscape.growthRate: P2P / community-event TAM growing ~8-12% YoY post-COVID; experiential spend up vs goods spend in 25-45 demographic; corporate offsite spend recovering to pre-2020 levels by 2025.
landscape.annualSGEvents: ~180,000 ticketed P2P + community events per year (estimated, organiser-side; excludes purely-private dinner parties)
landscape.escrowAdoption: <5% of SG event transactions use escrow today — almost all rely on instant PayNow + trust
landscape.vendorMarketplaceMaturity: Fragmented: Carousell Services, Bark, Fiverr each cover slices; no SG event-specialised vendor marketplace exists
landscape.theCommonsPositioning: P2P event hosting + escrow (funds released on event-day) + curated SG vendor marketplace, all on one platform — the only stack that solves discovery + payment-trust + vendor-sourcing together
keyTrends: Eventbrite remains default for SG semi-pro paid events but loses ground on small-ticket workshops to Luma and Peatix on fees
Luma adoption accelerating in SG tech / VC / hackathon community on UX strength alone
Klook + Airbnb Experiences own curated experience economy but stay closed to peer creators
PayNow + WhatsApp DIY remains the #1 silent competitor — 60%+ of SG P2P events still run on it
Carousell Services and Bark fragment the SG event-vendor sourcing problem; no consolidator
Escrow adoption near zero in SG events — strongest white-space dimension for The Commons
regulatoryNotes: MAS Payment Services Act (PSA) 2019 — escrow holding of customer funds requires either Major Payment Institution licence or partnering with a licensed e-money issuer / merchant acquirer (typical path: Stripe Connect Custom + ringfenced sub-accounts)
GST registration mandatory once annual taxable turnover crosses S$1M; SG corporate organisers expect GST/IRAS-ready invoices
PDPA — attendee personal data handling, opt-in consent for marketing communications
Tripartite Standards apply to vendor-side worker fairness if The Commons functions as principal employer for any vendor onboarding
landscape.seaContext: SE Asia P2P-event tooling is fragmented: Peatix (Japan/SG) holds community events, Klook (HK/SG) holds curated experiences, Eventbrite (US) holds semi-pro discovery. No SE Asia-native player has consolidated escrow + marketplace + discovery. The Commons targets this gap with daiquiri-beach branded SG-first UX.

commonWeaknesses: No event-day escrow on ticketing platforms — attendee bears cancellation risk
Vendor marketplace fragmented across Carousell / Bark / Fiverr / FunEmpire — no event-specialised consolidator
Fees compounded on small tickets (S$20-40) make Eventbrite uneconomic for community events
DIY (Google Form + PayNow + WhatsApp) silently dominates because no platform has solved organiser pain better
SG localisation gaps — most global platforms lack PayNow, GST/IRAS invoicing, multi-language UI
theCommonsAdvantages: Built-in event-day escrow — funds held until event delivery, attendees protected against cancellation
Curated SG event-vendor marketplace (photographers, caterers, MCs, decorators) integrated into the same platform
Daiquiri-beach branded UX — warm, tropical, social-first, distinct from Eventbrite/SISTIC corporate aesthetic
PayNow + SGD-default + GST/IRAS-ready from day one
Backed by Elitez Group — established SG operator stack provides KYC/payment-rail credibility
Flat 5% organiser fee + 10% provider booking fee — undercuts Eventbrite ~10.7% all-in on most ticket bands

borrowed: Luma — calendar-native invites and social-proof rails on event pages
Eventbrite — mature organiser tooling pattern (waitlists, promo codes, check-in)
Klook — mobile-first experience-card UX with clear pricing
Peerspace — escrow trust badges and host-buyer messaging UX
improved: True event-day escrow (vs Eventbrite's T+4-7 pay-out) — attendees protected, organisers credible
Integrated 2-sided vendor marketplace on the same surface (vs Carousell + Bark + Fiverr fragmentation)
Daiquiri-beach tropical brand voice — warm and social, not corporate-conference
SG localisation native (PayNow, SGD, GST) instead of bolt-on
Organiser pain solved end-to-end — discovery + RSVP + payment + escrow + vendor sourcing on one platform
